en.extract:
The white-throated pewee (Contopus albogularis) is a species of bird in the family Tyrannidae, the tyrant flycatchers. It is found in Brazil, French Guiana, and Suriname.


Taxonomy and systematics
The white-throated pewee was originally described as Myiochanes albogularis. The species is monotypic.


Description
The white-throated pewee is about 13 cm (5.1 in) long. The sexes have the same plumage. Adults have a dark blackish gray crown with a slight crest, slightly paler gray lores, and a thin white eye-ring on an otherwise dark sooty gray face. Their back is also dark sooty gray. Their wings are blackish brown and their tail blackish. Their throat is white and the rest of their underparts sooty gray. They have a dark iris, a wide flat bill with a black maxilla and yellow mandible, and black legs and feet.


Distribution and habitat
The white-throated pewee is found at scattered locations from eastern Suriname east through central French Guiana into far northeastern Brazil's Amapá state. It inhabits the edges and clearings of humid primary and secondary forest. In elevation it ranges between 400 and 700 m (1,300 and 2,300 ft).


Behavior


Movement
The white-throated pewee is a year-round resident.


Feeding
The white-throated pewee feeds on insects, though details are lacking. It sits erect on an exposed perch high in a tree and captures prey in mid-air with sallies from it ("hawking"). It usually returns to the same perch after a sally and "shivers" its tail upon landing.


Breeding
Nothing is known about the white-throated pewee's breeding biology.


Vocalization
The white-throated pewee's vocalizations are not well known. As of April 2025 xeno-canto and the Cornell Lab of Ornithology's Macaulay Library each had nine recordings with two of them in common. The species' call is described as a "high, dry wic-wic- -".


Status
The IUCN has assessed the white-throated pewee as being of Least Concern. Its population size is not known and is believed to be stable. No immediate threats have been identified. It is considered "uncommon and very local". It is common locally in Suriname's Brownsberg Nature Park.


References

The '''white-throated pewee''' ('''''Contopus albogularis''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Tyrannidae]], the tyrant flycatchers.<ref name=IOC15.1>{{cite web |url=https://www.worldbirdnames.org/new/bow/flycatchers/ |title=Tyrant flycatchers |website=IOC World Bird List |version =v 15.1 | editor1-last=Gill | editor1-first=Frank | editor1-link=Frank Gill (ornithologist) | editor2-last=Donsker | editor2-first=David | editor3-last=Rasmussen | editor3-first=Pamela | editor3-link=Pamela Rasmussen |date=March 2025 |access-date=March 3, 2025 }}</ref> It is found in [[Brazil]], [[French Guiana]], and [[Suriname]].<ref name=SACCcountries>Remsen, J. V., Jr., J. I. Areta, E. Bonaccorso, S. Claramunt, G. Del-Rio, A. Jaramillo, D. F. Lane, M. B. Robbins, F. G. Stiles, and K. J. Zimmer. Version 30 March 2025. Species Lists of Birds for South American Countries and Territories. https://www.museum.lsu.edu/~Remsen/SACCCountryLists.htm retrieved 30 March 2025</ref>

==Taxonomy and systematics==

The white-throated pewee was [[Species description|originally described]] as ''Myiochanes albogularis''.<ref>{{cite journal |last=Berlioz |first=J. |title=Étude d'une collection d'Oiseaux de Guyane française | journal=Bulletin du Muséum national d'histoire naturelle  | volume=34 | issue=2 | pages=138–139 | publisher=Imprimerie nationale | date= 1962 |language=French | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/54962873 |access-date=April 12, 2025 }}</ref> The species is [[monotypic]].<ref name=IOC15.1/>

==Description==

The white-throated pewee is about {{convert|13|cm|in|sigfig=2|abbr=on}} long. The sexes have the same plumage. Adults have a dark blackish gray crown with a slight crest, slightly paler gray [[Lore (anatomy)|lores]], and a thin white [[eye-ring]] on an otherwise dark sooty gray face. Their back is also dark sooty gray. Their wings are blackish brown and their tail blackish. Their throat is white and the rest of their underparts sooty gray. They have a dark iris, a wide flat bill with a black [[maxilla]] and yellow [[mandible]], and black legs and feet.<ref name=WTPE-BOW>Farnsworth, A. and D. J. Lebbin (2020). White-throated Pewee (''Contopus albogularis''), version 1.0. In Birds of the World (J. del Hoyo, A. Elliott, J. Sargatal, D. A. Christie, and E. de Juana, Editors). Cornell Lab of Ornithology, Ithaca, NY, USA. https://doi.org/10.2173/bow.whtpew1.01 retrieved April 12, 2025</ref>

==Distribution and habitat==

The white-throated pewee is found at scattered locations from eastern Suriname east through central French Guiana into far northeastern Brazil's [[Amapá]] state. It inhabits the edges and clearings of humid [[Primary forest|primary]] and [[Secondary forest|secondary]] forest. In elevation it ranges between {{convert|400|and|700|m|ft|-2|abbr=on}}.<ref name=WTPE-BOW/><ref name=vanPerlo>{{cite book | last =van Perlo | first = Ber| title =A Field Guide to the Birds of Brazil | publisher =Oxford University Press | date =2009 | location =New York | pages =316–317 | isbn =978-0-19-530155-7 }}</ref>

==Behavior==
===Movement===

The white-throated pewee is a year-round resident.<ref name=WTPE-BOW/>

===Feeding===

The white-throated pewee feeds on insects, though details are lacking. It sits erect on an exposed perch high in a tree and captures prey in mid-air with sallies from it ("[[Hawking (birds)|hawking]]"). It usually returns to the same perch after a sally and "shivers" its tail upon landing.<ref name=WTPE-BOW/>

===Breeding===

Nothing is known about the white-throated pewee's breeding biology.<ref name=WTPE-BOW/>

{{birdsong|url=https://xeno-canto.org/species/Contopus-albogularis |species=the white-throated pewee}}
===Vocalization===

The white-throated pewee's vocalizations are not well known. As of April 2025 [[xeno-canto]] and the [[Cornell Lab of Ornithology]]'s [[Macaulay Library]] each had nine recordings with two of them in common.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://xeno-canto.org/species/Contopus-albogularis |title=White-throated Pewee ''Contopus albogularis'' |author=<!--Not stated--> |date=April 12, 2025 |website=xeno-canto  |access-date=April 12, 2025 }}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://search.macaulaylibrary.org/catalog?taxonCode=whtpew1&mediaType=audio&sort=rating_rank_desc |title=White-throated Pewee ''Contopus albogularis'' |author=<!--Not stated--> |date=April 12, 2025 |website=Macaulay Library |access-date=April 12, 2025 }}</ref> The species' call is described as a "high, dry ''wic-wic- -''".<ref name=vanPerlo/>

==Status==

The [[IUCN]] has assessed the white-throated pewee as being of Least Concern. Its population size is not known and is believed to be stable. No immediate threats have been identified.<ref name=IUCN/> It is considered "uncommon and very local". It is common locally in Suriname's [[Brownsberg Nature Park]].<ref name=WTPE-BOW/>
